dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3089 IS: Secure Takedown and Obstruction Prevention Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Moreno introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend section 111 of title 18, United States Code, to prohibit barricading while evading arrest.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Secure Takedown and Obstruction Prevention Act or the STOP Act.

paragraph.text: Law enforcement officers across the United States face unprecedented challenges and dangers in the execution of their duties to protect communities, enforce laws, and maintain public order.

Barricading actions often escalate confrontations, prolong dangerous standoffs, divert critical resources, and increase the risk of serious physical harm or death to officers, suspects, and innocent bystanders.

Federal law enforcement officers make profound sacrifices every day, including risking their lives to serve and protect the people of the United States. Their unwavering dedication deserves the full support and protection of Federal law.

section.header: Offense of barricading during arrest evasion
section.text: Section 111 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (c) as subsection (d); and
by inserting after subsection (b) the following:

clause.text: taking a position in a physical location that prevents immediate access by any Federal law enforcement officer; and

refusing and resisting order to exit the location, or comply with other lawful direction, when the person knows or reasonably should know that the Federal law enforcement officer is attempting to apprehend the person.

subparagraph.text: The term Federal law enforcement officer has the meaning given the term in section 115.

paragraph.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to—

subparagraph.text: engage in barricading in the course of forcibly resisting a Federal law enforcement officer engaged in the performance of official duties, in violation of subsection (a)(1); or
aid, assist, or attempt to aid or assist another person in committing conduct described in subparagraph (A).
shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 3 years, or both; or
shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both, if, during the commission of a violation of paragraph (2)—

clause.text: the violation creates a risk of or causes serious physical harm to any person;
the person has possession or claims possession of a deadly weapon; or
a third party is present and is unable to immediately and safely leave the physical location of the violation.
